brief_document.title: Workflow-management handler layer over the Engine
brief_document.purpose: Implement the single handler layer that translates each workflow-management wire request into Engine calls. This is the pure-adapter core: deserialise, scope the namespace, call exactly one Engine method, serialise the result. No orchestration, no durable decision — that all stays in the engine.
brief_document.task: Implement a transport-independent handler layer with one handler per workflow-management operation (start/signal/query/cancel/list/describe). Each handler takes the deserialised wire request, obtains a namespace-scoped engine handle via the AW-007 guard, calls exactly one (or a small fixed composition of) Engine method(s), and returns the wire response or a mapped WireError. The handlers are shared by both the gRPC and HTTP transports (AW-009).

requirements.title: Pure-adapter handlers
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement one handler per workflow-management operation that deserialises the request, scopes the namespace via the guard, calls the matching Engine method, and serialises the result, performing no retry, scheduling, sequencing, or other durable decision.
acceptance: handlers.rs defines start/signal/query/cancel/list/describe handlers, each transport-independent (taking the decoded request + the scoped engine handle).

start -> Engine::start_workflow, signal -> Engine::signal, query -> Engine::query, cancel -> Engine::cancel, list -> Engine::list_workflows (via WorkflowFilter), describe -> a WorkflowSummary (and optional history from the store).
No handler retries, schedules, sequences, or composes multiple workflow operations with server-side decision logic (per D5); each is a thin translation.
An inspection/review confirms no handler contains a loop, retry, or branch that constitutes a durable decision (CO6).

requirements.title: Namespace scoping and error mapping in every handler
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL route every handler through the AW-007 guard before calling the Engine, and SHALL map every Engine/store failure onto a WireError, never exposing engine internals.
acceptance: Every handler calls the namespace guard first and aborts with NamespaceDenied if authorisation fails, before any Engine call (CO9).
Engine and store errors are mapped to WireError via the AW-002 mapping; no Debug/Display of an internal error crosses the handler boundary (D3).
A unit test drives each handler against an InMemoryStore-backed engine (aion-engine CO13) asserting the correct Engine method is invoked and the result/error is mapped correctly.
A test asserts a handler returns NamespaceDenied without calling the engine when the guard denies.

boundaries: SHALL NOT bind a transport (tonic/axum) — that is AW-009; these handlers are transport-independent.
SHALL NOT implement WebSocket subscription or worker dispatch — AW-010, AW-011.
SHALL NOT add orchestration or durable decisions (CO6, D5).
verification: Tests run against InMemoryStore — no database required.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.

checklist.text: A single handler layer translates each workflow-management request into exactly one (or a small fixed composition of) Engine call(s); it performs no retry, scheduling, sequencing, or other durable decision.

stories.text: As a workflow caller, I want to start, signal, query, cancel, list, and describe workflows over gRPC or HTTP so that I can drive the engine from any service without embedding it.

constraints.text: No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] lint bypasses per CLAUDE.md; tests needing a running server gate at runtime (a *_TEST_URL env var with a logged skip), never #[ignore].
lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
aion-proto is a leaf crate: it depends on aion-core and external crates only — no aion, no aion-server, no store backend. Structural; must hold (per D2).
aion-server depends on aion, aion-proto, and a store backend among workspace crates; it contains no workflow execution logic — it delegates every durable decision to the Engine (per D5).
Wire data types (proto messages, streamed events, summaries) derive Serialize + Deserialize; config types that carry secrets are Deserialize-only (D4, project credential policy).
Library errors use thiserror (WireError in proto, ServerError in server); anyhow appears only in aion-server's top-level main. No unwrap/expect in library code; lock/stream poison handled explicitly.
No cross-namespace operation reaches the engine: namespace authorisation and scoping happen at the adapter boundary before any Engine call (per D8).
A slow or stalled WebSocket consumer is dropped with a typed lag error; per-connection buffers are bounded; the engine broadcast is never back-pressured by a subscriber (per D6).
No hardcoded operational defaults (ports, heartbeat window, buffer bound, timeouts): all from config; engine-owned defaults deferred to the engine (per CLAUDE.md 'no assumed defaults').
The wire Event/WorkflowFilter/WorkflowSummary/WorkflowStatus and ID types are aion-core's types, not re-declared wire clones (per D2).

resolved_context.intention: Build the network skin of Aion. The aion engine crate is transport-agnostic by design — no HTTP, gRPC, or WebSocket — so a CLI embeds it with nothing but a file-backed store. Teams running many services want a managed deployment: one binary clients and workers point at, that streams live workflow events to a dashboard, isolates tenants, and dispatches activity tasks to out-of-process workers in any language. That is aion-server, and the wire contract it speaks is aion-proto. aion-proto is a pure leaf-level contract — gRPC service definitions plus serde wire types — that the server, every client SDK (AL), and every worker SDK (AR) agree on, defined once so they never drift. aion-server is the deployable: it wraps a live Engine, translates wire requests into Engine calls, tails the engine's event broadcast to feed WebSocket subscribers, runs the server side of the remote-worker protocol, partitions everything by namespace, and serves the dashboard's API and static assets. It adds no execution logic — every durable decision still happens in aion.
